sanskrit:
क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा।
भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम्॥
transliteration:
kṣetra-kṣetrajñayor evam antaraṃ jñāna-cakṣuṣā |
bhūta-prakṛti-mokṣaṃ ca ye vidur yānti te param ||
literal_meaning: Those who, with the eye of knowledge, perceive the distinction between the field and the knower of the field, and also understand the liberation of beings from material nature, they attain the supreme.
contextual_meaning: True freedom arises when one experientially distinguishes the eternal conscious witness from the ever-changing personality and world, ending compulsive identification with mind-body processes.
anchor_text: Witness consciousness dis-identifies from mind-body, ending compulsive patterns, reaching inner freedom.
psychological_state: Post-insight integration; observer stance stabilizes, reducing fusion with thoughts and emotions.
trigger_scenario: Client trapped in rumination or ego-stories learns to label experiences as mental events, not identity.
